---
title: "DA_Project"
author: "Alex Copeland"
format: 
  embed-resources: TRUE
editor: visual
---

```{R}
#| echo: false

# Reading in dataset

hotels <- readr::read_csv('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2020/2020-02-11/hotels.csv', show_col_types = FALSE)
```

```{R}
# Defining Variables

# Subset dataset by hotel type
CityHotel <- hotels[hotels$hotel == "City Hotel" , ]
ResortHotel <- hotels[hotels$hotel == "Resort Hotel" , ]

# Define vector of predictor names for regression
characteristics <- c("adults", "children", "babies", "lead_time", "stays_in_weekend_nights", "stays_in_week_nights")

# for regression
unique_characteristics <- list()

```

```{R}
# Question One
# Do city hotels or resort hotels have more children per booking?

t.test(CityHotel$children, ResortHotel$children)

# There is a statistical significant difference between children per type of hotel. On average city hotels have .03 - .04 less children per booking.

```



```{R}
# Question Two
# Regression model predicting the average daily rate
lm_adr <- lm(adr ~ adults + children + babies + lead_time + stays_in_weekend_nights + stays_in_week_nights, data = hotels)

# Regression model by City Hotel
lm_ch_adr <- lm(adr ~ adults + children + babies + lead_time + stays_in_weekend_nights + stays_in_week_nights, data = CityHotel)

# Regression model by Resort Hotel
lm_rh_adr <- lm(adr ~ adults + children + babies + lead_time + stays_in_weekend_nights + stays_in_week_nights, data = ResortHotel)

# OUTPUT NOTES
# summary(lm_adr)
# Coefficients:
# adults 19.8
# children  39.6
# babies 7.7

# Each adult adds an additional $19.8 to the average daily rate
# each child adds 39.6 (I assume this is because the adults mainly need one bed, and the children would require more beds meaning a more expensive room?)
# stays_in_week_nights adds $1.1 to the average daily rate
# each weekend night decreases adr by 0.5, but is not significant indicating no relationship between the adr and weekends

# Do the same for CityHotel and ResortHotel for model by hotel types











# combn function (https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/r-language/calculate-combinations-and-permutations-in-r/)
# loop list explanation (https://stackoverflow.com/questions/26508519/how-to-add-elements-to-a-list-in-r-loop)

# Unique combinations of characteristics

list_add <- list()
unique_characteristics <- list()
for (i in 1:6){
  
  list_add <- combn(characteristics, i, simplify = FALSE) #FALSE makes this a list
  
  unique_characteristics[[i]] <- list_add
}







lm_1predictor <- function(list, dv = "adr") {
  
  results <- list()
  
  for (i in 1:length(list[[1]])){
  
    lm_mod1 <- lm(dv ~ list[[i]])
    results[[i]] <- lm_mod1
    
  }
}

lm_1predictor(unique_characteristics)












```















## Introduction

This project blah blah

#### Dataset

The dataset used for this project is the Hotels dataset from the tidytuesday GitHub repository.

# maybe more ?s
food for week vs weekend (ttest)

food per months (anova?)

Do repeated guest stay longer? (ttest)

Do stays_in_week_nights collectively make up more than stays_in_weekend_nights?
